audioIngestion.label: Audio Ingestion
audioIngestion.description: Upload and ingest forensic audio files

validation.unsupportedFormat: Unsupported audio format. Supported formats: WAV, MP3, M4A
validation.fileTooLarge: File is too large. Maximum file size is 2GB

errors.uploadFailed: Failed to upload file. Please try again.
errors.onlyOneFile: You can only upload one file at a time.
errors.onlyOneFileAtATime: A file is being processed. Please wait until it finishes before uploading another one.

audioIngestion.title: Ingest Audio Evidence
audioIngestion.description: Securely upload and catalog forensic audio files for authentication and enhancement.
audioIngestion.dragDrop: Drag and drop audio files here
audioIngestion.uploadInfo: Files will be automatically analyzed using pattern and metadata forensics to detect potential AI-generated or manipulated audio. Analysis runs in the background without affecting upload speed.
audioIngestion.highNoiseText: Detects padding sequences and null patterns that reveal synthetic processing. Identifies micro-structural repetitions characteristic of AI voice engines.
audioIngestion.batchText: Inspects inconsistencies in internal records and container architecture indicating manipulation, re-encoding, or third-party software use.

metadata.title: Metadata module
metadata.description: Here you can centralise forms, results and tools related to audio metadata analysis.
metadata.noFileMessage: Load a file in the audio ingestion section first.

patterns.title: Pattern Analysis
patterns.description: Analyze binary patterns and padding in audio files. Adjust the bitmap width to observe different pattern scales.
patterns.noFileMessage: Load a file in the audio ingestion section first.
patterns.invalidWidth: Width must be between 128 and 2048 bytes
patterns.autosimilarityDesc: Autosimilitude analysis: Grayscale representation of byte values. Patterns reveal the statistical structure and characteristics of the audio data.
patterns.paddingDesc: Inspect the first and last 1024 bytes of the file to detect padding (0x00 or 0xFF) that may indicate file manipulation or encoding artifacts.
